tagline: An undisciplined, unrelenting, and unqualified cabaret-style night of performance.
venueAddress: 317 East Houston Street, New York, NY 10002
accessibilityNote:
The bar is on the ground floor. The performance venue and accessible bathrooms are accessed via a portable ramp (1.5 steps).
Reserved seating available for accessibility needs upon request.
aboutText:
Unprofessional is a quarterly night of cabaret, where you never quite know what you'll get. Launched on Maya Suess's birthday in February 2022, the show lives in the back room of The Parkside Lounge on Houston and Attorney in the Lower East Side.

The programming is truly intergenerational, introducing younger performers to the legendary performers from the neighborhood. From Coney Island legends to drag kings, from performance artists to folk musicians, every edition is a different world.

Auntie Climax - Dance Party at Parkside

aboutDetails.title: The Venue
aboutDetails.text: The Parkside Lounge. A dive bar and performance venue catering to the artists, musicians, and freaks of the Lower East Side since 1908.

aboutDetails.title: The Emcee + Producer
aboutDetails.text: Maya Suess, Born on a small Cascadian island, now barely leaves the LES in NYC, Lenapehoking. She gives money to NYC dancers by day and makes drawings, performances, and other mischievous entities by night.

aboutDetails.title: The Pianist
aboutDetails.text: Klovis Gaynor accompanies our emcee at nearly every edition; A Queens-based, Queer musician and "baroque punk" artist.

aboutDetails.title: The Band
aboutDetails.text: Libby, Soft World Maker, makes our sets. Vandy runs our tech, + Lorenzo, shoots video (the Vanburgo Collective). Tata takes photos. Macauley holds the Gate. The Parkside Lounge staff keeps us in joyful chaos. David Bullen hair meistro.

aboutDetails.title: The Looks
aboutDetails.text: Maya's looks are provided by Maegan and the team at East Village Vintage. Providing a curated collection at thrift store prices.

aboutDetails.title: The Deal
aboutDetails.text: Sliding scale cover. All monies go directly to the performers. Tickets through Humanitix, where 100% of booking fees go to charity.

aboutQuote: "Allied Productions exemplifies one of the things I love about this neighborhood, which is the diverse legacy of experimental queer and radical art and performance. At the Unprofessional Variety Show, I try to make the programming truly intergenerational."
contactCta: Want to perform? Have a wild idea? Just want to be part of whatever this is? Reach out. The show is built by its community, and the door is always open.
footer2: Est. 2022 - The Parkside Lounge, LES NYC
